pattern: Dijkstra's shortest-path algorithm from the source node gives the time each node first receives the signal; the answer is the largest of those arrival times.
hints: The signal reaches each node along the fastest cumulative path from the start.
This is single-source shortest paths on a weighted directed graph.
Run Dijkstra from k with a min-heap; if any node is unreachable return -1, otherwise return the maximum finalized distance.

edge_inputs.description: statement example two single edge reachable
shorter alternate path beats direct edge

stress.description: random reachable graph built from a spanning tree plus extra edges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(60, size))
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    nodes = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(nodes)
    if nodes[0] != k:
        idx = nodes.index(k)
        nodes[0], nodes[idx] = nodes[idx], nodes[0]
    edges = set()
    times = []
    for i in range(1, len(nodes)):
        u = nodes[rng.randint(0, i - 1)]
        v = nodes[i]
        edges.add((u, v))
        times.append([u, v, rng.randint(0, 100)])
    extra = rng.randint(0, n)
    for _ in range(extra):
        u = rng.randint(1, n)
        v = rng.randint(1, n)
        if u != v and (u, v) not in edges:
            edges.add((u, v))
            times.append([u, v, rng.randint(0, 100)])
    if not times:
        times.append([k, k % n + 1 if n > 1 else k, 0])
        if n == 1:
            times = [[1, 1, 0]]
    return [times, n, k]
